Dietitian
Licensed Dietitian (LD) Guide

Issued by the Alabama Board of Examiners for Dietetics/Nutrition Practice under the Independent State Board. Data verified for 2026.

Total Initial Fee
$250
$100 application fee + $150 initial license fee
Renewal
$200
Every 2 years + 30 CE hours
Processing Time
2 to 4 Weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 Years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Alabama Board of Examiners for Dietetics/Nutrition Practice

Montgomery, AL 36130

Physical Address

400 South Union Street, Suite 445
Montgomery, AL 36104

Phone & Fax

Phone: (334) 242-4505

Fax:

Email

Credential Categories

Alabama Dietitian License Types

Licensed Dietitian (LD) / Licensed Nutritionist (LN)

The standard credential to practice dietetics in Alabama. Alabama law protects both the 'Dietitian' and 'Nutritionist' titles.

Temporary License

Available for applicants who have met the educational and supervised practice requirements and are preparing to take the CDR exam. Valid for one year. Fee is $175.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Alabama Licensed Dietitian (LD)

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,200 hours of supervised practice.

Pass the CDR Examination

Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) national examination.

Submit the Application

Complete the licensure application and submit it to the Board with the $100 application fee and $150 initial license fee ($250 total).

Provide Verification

Submit a copy of your current, active CDR registration card or verification letter directly from CDR.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • CDR Registration VerificationProof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Driver's License or Government IDVerification of identity and legal presence in the U.S.
  • Official TranscriptsSent directly from the university showing your conferred degree.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

Alabama offers a temporary license for $175 for individuals who have completed their education and supervised practice but have not yet passed the CDR exam. It is valid for one year.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Alabama allows licensure by endorsement for dietitians who hold a current, valid license in another state with requirements equivalent to or stricter than Alabama's, provided the applicant holds active CDR registration.

Frequently Asked Questions

Alabama Dietitian FAQ

How much does an Alabama dietitian license cost?

The initial cost is $250, which includes a non-refundable $100 application fee and a $150 license fee. The temporary license costs $175.

When does the Alabama license need to be renewed?

The license is renewed biennially (every two years). The renewal fee is $200.

What are the continuing education requirements?

Alabama requires 30 hours of continuing professional education (CPE) during each two-year renewal cycle.
